details: Through the “Skill Development Fee Reimbursement Scheme”, the SPC will reimburse 50% of the fees up to ₹ 50,000/- to the students who have successfully completed an SPC-approved technical skill training program in any of the approved domains from a Goan educational institute.
benefits: Reimbursement of 50% of the fees up to ₹ 50,000/- to the students who have successfully completed an SPC-approved technical skill training program in any of the "
", attended in a Goan educational institute:
Up to 1000 students per year can avail the benefits of this scheme. However, this cap can be reviewed and revised based on the need in future.
A student can avail the benefits of this scheme for any two courses in a year.
Under no circumstance shall the benefits under this scheme be considered an entitlement. The SPC shall reserve the sole right to accept or reject applications.
eligibility: 1. The applicant should be an educational institution.
2. The educational institute must be incorporated in Goa or have a registered place of business in Goa.
3. The technical skill training program must be approved by SPC and should fall under any of the technical domains:

Internet of things, Artificial intelligence, Machine Learning, Interaction Design, Big Data Analytics, Robotics, 3-D Printing, Data Science, Drones, Virtual Reality/Augmented Reality/Merged Reality, Others (subject to approval from SPC).
4. The students who are currently pursuing their graduation or are within two years from receiving their graduate degree within the original stipulated period of study can enrol for these programs.

Only expenditures incurred after notification of Goa Start-up Policy 2017, being within the validity of this policy and paid for digitally will be considered for reimbursements under this scheme. In case digital payments are not possible, then it shall be up to the decision of SPC as per its due diligence to admit the expenditure.
For the purpose of this scheme “
” means an institute registered or operating in Goa.
For the purpose of this scheme the term “
” refers to a person satisfying at least one of the following criteria:
a) Person born in the State of Goa and studying in Goa or outside Goa; or,
b) Person having domicile for 10 years or more in the State and studying in Goa or outside Goa; or,
c) Person who is not a State domicile but is studying in the educational institutes in the State.

online: Verify your Email ID and Mobile Number using OTP. You will be redirected to the Registration Page.
, fill in all the mandatory fields of the registration form (Country, Name, Date of Birth, Address, PIN Code, Gender, etc.).
Create a Login Name and a strong Password (Password can have special characters like @ # $ % ^ & + =).
Carefully read the Declaration and the Terms & Conditions, and tick the checkboxes.
Fill in the Captcha Code, and click "Register/Signup".
Login to the website using your Login Name and Password. Fill in the Captcha Code, and click "Login".
Navigate to the online application form for the relevant scheme.
In the application form, fill in all the mandatory fields and upload all the mandatory documents (self-attest if required).
Submit the application and note the application reference number for application tracking.
of the application form. Fill in all the mandatory fields, and attach copies of all the mandatory documents (self-attest if required).
Submit the duly filled and signed application form along with the documents to SPC via Email at spc-dit.goa@gov.in
The applicant can apply for this scheme at any time of the financial year but only within 6 months of incurring the relevant expenditure.
The applications received by the SPC shall be evaluated and approval or rejection shall be notified within 45 days from receipt of the application by the SPC.

documents_required: Copy of SPC’s approval of the program*
The form and documents as mentioned in Annexure 1*

faqs.question: What happens if false information is provided in the application?
faqs.answer: If false information or fraudulent activity is found in connection with the application, the SPC may prohibit the applicant from availing of the scheme benefits.

faqs.question: What documents are required for claiming the incentive?
faqs.answer: The mandatory documents include Aadhaar card of the student, course completion certificate, copy of SPC's approval of the program, original proof of payment/fees receipt, and the completed application form.

faqs.question: How long does it take to process and approve applications?
faqs.answer: The SPC evaluates and notifies approval or rejection within 45 days from receipt of the application, and once approved, the amount is disbursed within 60 days from the date of approval.

faqs.question: Is there a specific time frame for applying for this scheme?
faqs.answer: Applicants can apply for this scheme at any time during the financial year, but only within 6 months of incurring the relevant expenditure.

faqs.question: Who has the authority to accept or reject claims filed by students?
faqs.answer: The SPC has the sole authority to accept or reject claims filed by students, and its decision is final and binding.

faqs.question: What expenses are considered for reimbursement under this scheme?
faqs.answer: Only expenses incurred after the notification of Goa Start-up Policy 2017, paid digitally, and within the policy's validity will be considered for reimbursement.

faqs.question: Can students currently pursuing graduation or those within two years of receiving their graduate degree apply for these programs?
faqs.answer: Yes, students within two years of completing their graduate degree can enroll for these programs.

faqs.question: What are the eligibility criteria for educational institutes?
faqs.answer: Educational institutes must be incorporated in Goa or have a registered place of business in Goa.

faqs.question: Who qualifies as a "student" under this scheme?
faqs.answer: A "student" can be someone born in Goa studying in Goa or elsewhere, a person with domicile in Goa for at least 10 years studying in Goa or elsewhere, or a person studying in educational institutes in Goa even if not a State domicile.

faqs.question: What is the definition of a "Goan educational institute" for this scheme?
faqs.answer: A "Goan educational institute" refers to an institute registered or operating in Goa.

faqs.question: Is the scheme an entitlement for all eligible students?
faqs.answer: No, the benefits are not considered entitlements. The Startup Promotion Cell (SPC) reserves the right to accept or reject applications.

faqs.question: Can a student avail of the benefits for more than two courses in a year?
faqs.answer: No, a student can only avail of the benefits for a maximum of two courses in a year.

faqs.question: How many students can benefit from this scheme each year?
faqs.answer: Up to 1000 students can benefit from this scheme annually, but this cap can be reviewed and revised if necessary.
